popularStory.headline: Memorial
popularStory.description:
Margaret de Monthermer was born on 14 Oct 1329 in Saint Humbert, Stokenham, Devonshire, England. She died on 24 Mar 1394/1395 and is buried in Salisbury Cathedral, Salisbury, Wiltshire, England.

zh.lifeSummary: 當Margaret de Monthermer於1329年10月14日出生在英國戴文斯托肯哈姆時，她的父親Sir Thomas de Monthermer 2nd Baron de Monthermer28歲，母親Margaret de Braose24歲。她於1350年7月2日與John Montagu在英國戴文斯托肯哈姆結婚。他們至少是4個兒子和4個女兒的父母。她去世於1394年3月24日在英國英格蘭維爾爾享年64歲，埋葬在英國英格蘭維爾爾索爾茲伯里。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Margaret de Monthermer于1329年10月14日出生在英国戴文斯托肯哈姆时，她的父亲Sir Thomas de Monthermer 2nd Baron de Monthermer28岁，母亲Margaret de Braose24岁。她于1350年7月2日与John Montagu在英国戴文斯托肯哈姆结婚。他们至少是4个儿子和4个女儿的父母。她去世于1394年3月24日在英国英格兰維爾爾享年64岁，埋葬在英国英格兰維爾爾索爾茲伯里。

variants.name: Dey
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Ashit, Indu, Naina, Partha, Soumya, Ajit, Anjan, Anjana, Anup, Arjun, Arun, Ashim.
English, Scottish, and Welsh: variant of   Day  .
German: topographic name from a field name in North Rhine-Westphalia, denoting a sizeable piece of land, from Middle Low German dē ‘thigh’ (referring to the shape of the property).

nameMeanings.name: Margaret
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: An extremely common given name from the Middle Ages onwards, derived via Old French Marguerite and Latin Margarita from Greek Margarītēs, from margaron ‘pearl’, a word ultimately of Hebrew origin. The name was always understood to mean ‘pearl’ throughout the Middle Ages. The first St Margaret was martyred at Antioch in Pisidia during the persecution instigated by the Emperor Diocletian in the early 4th century. However, there is some doubt about her name, as the same saint is venerated in the Orthodox Church as Marina . There were several other saintly bearers of the name, including St Margaret of Scotland ( d. 1093 ), wife of King Malcolm Canmore and daughter of Edmund Ironside of England. It was also the name of the wife of Henry VI of England, Margaret of Anjou ( 1430–82 ), and of Margaret Tudor ( 1489–1541 ), sister of Henry VIII , who married James IV of Scotland and ruled as regent there after his death. See also Margery , Marjorie .

variants.name: Margareta
meanings: From the Latin form of Greek Margarītēs. See Margaret .

variants.name: Margaretta
meanings: An extremely common given name from the Middle Ages onwards, derived via Old French Marguerite and Latin Margarita from Greek Margarītēs, from margaron ‘pearl’, a word ultimately of Hebrew origin. The name was always understood to mean ‘pearl’ throughout the Middle Ages. The first St Margaret was martyred at Antioch in Pisidia during the persecution instigated by the Emperor Diocletian in the early 4th century. However, there is some doubt about her name, as the same saint is venerated in the Orthodox Church as Marina . There were several other saintly bearers of the name, including St Margaret of Scotland ( d. 1093 ), wife of King Malcolm Canmore and daughter of Edmund Ironside of England. It was also the name of the wife of Henry VI of England, Margaret of Anjou ( 1430–82 ), and of Margaret Tudor ( 1489–1541 ), sister of Henry VIII , who married James IV of Scotland and ruled as regent there after his death. See also Margery , Marjorie .

historicEvents.title: The Hundred Years' War
historicEvents.description: The Hundred Years' War between France and England was largely due to the question of legitimate succession to the French crown.

historicEvents.title: Wycliffe's Bible
historicEvents.description: John Wycliffe, English theologian and professor at the University of Oxford, completed a translation of the Bible from the Latin Vulgate into Middle English. This translation is known as Wycliffe's Bible and was completed around 1382.

en.lifeSummary: When Margaret de Monthermer was born on 14 October 1329, in Stokenham, Devon, England, her father, Sir Thomas de Monthermer 2nd Baron de Monthermer, was 28 and her mother, Margaret de Braose, was 24. She married John Montagu on 2 July 1350, in Stokenham, Devon, England. They were the parents of at least 4 sons and 4 daughters. She died on 24 March 1394, in Wiltshire, England, United Kingdom, at the age of 64, and was buried in Salisbury, Wiltshire, England, United Kingdom.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Margaret de Monthermer est née le 14 octobre 1329, à Stokenham, Devon, Angleterre, son père, Sir Thomas de Monthermer 2nd Baron de Monthermer, avait 28 ans et sa mère, Margaret de Braose, avait 24 ans. Elle a épousé John Montagu le 2 juillet 1350, à Stokenham, Devon, Angleterre. Ils ont eu au moins 4 fils et 4 filles. Elle est décédée le 24 mars 1394, à Wiltshire, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni, à l’âge de 64 ans, et a été enterrée à Salisbury, Wiltshire, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni.

de.lifeSummary: Als Margaret de Monthermer am 14. Oktober 1329 in Stokenham, Devon, England geboren wurde, war ihr Vater Sir Thomas de Monthermer 2nd Baron de Monthermer 28 und ihre Mutter Margaret de Braose 24. Sie hat am 2. Juli 1350 in Stokenham, Devon, England John Montagu geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 4 Söhne und 4 Töchter. Sie starb am 24. März 1394 im Alter von 64 Jahren in Wiltshire, England, United Kingdom und wurde in Salisbury, Wiltshire, England, United Kingdom begraben.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Margaret de Monthermer è nata il il 14 ottobre 1329 nel Stokenham, Devon, England, sua padre, Sir Thomas de Monthermer 2nd Baron de Monthermer, aveva 28 anni e sua madre, Margaret de Braose, aveva 24 anni. Lei sposato John Montagu il 2 luglio 1350, nel Stokenham, Devon, England. Erano genitori di almeno 4 figli e 4 figlie. Morì il 24 marzo 1394, nel Wiltshire, Inghilterra, Regno Unito, all'età di 64 anni, e fu sepolta a Salisbury, Wiltshire, Inghilterra, Regno Unito.

ja.lifeSummary: Margaret de Monthermerが1329年10月14日にイギリスデボンストケンハムで生まれた時，父Sir Thomas de Monthermer 2nd Baron de Monthermerは28歳，母Margaret de Braoseは24歳であった。1350年7月2日にイギリスデボンストケンハムでJohn Montaguと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも4人の息子と4人の娘の親であった。彼女は1394年3月24日にイギリスイギリスウィルトシュアイアで64歳で死去し，イギリスイギリスウィルトシュアイアサリスブリーにて埋葬された。

ko.lifeSummary: Margaret de Monthermer(이)가 1329년 10월 14일 영국 데폰 스토켄함에서 태어났을 때, 그녀의 아버지 Sir Thomas de Monthermer 2nd Baron de Monthermer(은)는 28세였고, 그녀의 어머니 Margaret de Braose(은)는 24세였다. 그녀는 1350년 7월 2일 영국 데폰 스토켄함에서 John Montagu(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 네 명의 아들과 네 명의 딸을 두었다. 그녀는 1394년 3월 24일 영국 잉글랜드 윌츠히르에서 64세의 나이로 사망했으며 영국 잉글랜드 윌츠히르 살리스버이에 묻혔다.

pt.14 October 1329–24 March 1394: 14 de outubro de 1329–24 de março de 1394
pt.lifeSummary: Quando Margaret de Monthermer nasceu em 14 de outubro de 1329, em Stokenham, Devon, Inglaterra, seu pai, Sir Thomas de Monthermer 2nd Baron de Monthermer, tinha 28 anos e sua mãe, Margaret de Braose, tinha 24 anos. Ela casou-se com John Montagu em 2 de julho de 1350, em Stokenham, Devon, Inglaterra. Eles tiveram pelo menos 4 filhos e 4 filhas. Ela faleceu em 24 de março de 1394, em Wiltshire, Inglaterra, United Kingdom, com 64 anos, e foi sepultada em Salisbury, Wiltshire, Inglaterra, United Kingdom.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Margaret de Monthermer родилась 14 октября 1329 г. в местности стокенам, девон, Англия, её отцу по имени Sir Thomas de Monthermer 2nd Baron de Monthermer было 28 лет, а матери, которую звали Margaret de Braose, 24. Она вышла замуж за человека по имени John Montagu 2 июля 1350 г. в местности стокенам, девон, Англия. У них было по крайней мере 4 сына и 4 дочери. Она умерла 24 марта 1394 г. в местности уилцхир, Англия, Великобритания в возрасте 64 лет и была похоронена в местности салисбури, уилцхир, Англия, Великобритания.

es.14 October 1329–24 March 1394: 14 de octubre de 1329–24 de marzo de 1394
es.lifeSummary: Cuando Margaret de Monthermer nació el 14 de octubre de 1329, en Stokenham, Devon, Inglaterra, su papá, Sir Thomas de Monthermer 2nd Baron de Monthermer, tenía 28 años y su mamá, Margaret de Braose, tenía 24 años. Se casó con John Montagu el 2 de julio de 1350, en Stokenham, Devon, Inglaterra. Fueron padres de por lo menos 4 hijos y 4 hijas. Murió el 24 de marzo de 1394, en Wiltshire, Inglaterra, Reino Unido, a la edad de 64 años, y fue sepultada en Salisbury, Wiltshire, Inglaterra, Reino Unido.
